Pseudoperonospora cubensis.distribution: widespread eastern US; overwinters only in the frost-free South (S. Florida, S. Texas) and disperses north each season — Southeast is the perennial core
Pseudoperonospora cubensis.quote: P. cubensis overwinters on infected cucurbits ... in areas that do not experience a hard frost, such as southern Florida in the eastern United States ... and is spread north in wind and storms.

Colletotrichum orbiculare.distribution: occurs worldwide; common throughout the humid eastern US (Florida, KY, MA, MN), sporadic in arid regions (New Mexico) — humid-favoring, present across the South
Colletotrichum orbiculare.quote: Anthracnose on cucurbits, caused by Colletotrichum orbiculare, is a common disease that occurs worldwide and throughout Florida ... Frequent rains, warm temperatures (74-78F), and high humidity favor anthracnose.
